zh.Llys Y, Flintshire, Wales: 英國威爾斯福林爾利斯 Y 可德
zh.lifeSummary: 當Lluddocca ap Tudor Trevor , Lord of Maelors and Oswestry於大約0934年出生在威爾士丹比爾時，他的父親King Tudor Trevor of Hereford35歲，母親Angharad ferch Hywel35歲。他於0979年與Angharad ferch Iago在威爾士福林爾弗林特結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1037年在英國威爾斯丹比爾可享年104歲。

zh-hans.Whittington, Shropshire, England: 英国英格兰史洛普爾廷頓
zh-hans.Llys Y, Flintshire, Wales: 英国威尔斯福林爾利斯 Y 可德
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Lluddocca ap Tudor Trevor , Lord of Maelors and Oswestry于大约0934年出生在威尔士丹比爾时，他的父亲King Tudor Trevor of Hereford35岁，母亲Angharad ferch Hywel35岁。他于0979年与Angharad ferch Iago在威尔士福林爾弗林特结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他去世于1037年在英国威尔斯丹比爾可享年104岁。

nameMeanings.name: ap
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Dutch: occupational name for a cobbler.
Slovenian: nickname for a talkative person, from lapati ‘to babble’, or a topographic name from dialect lap ‘abyss’. Compare Lapp 4.

variants.name: Lapp
meanings: German, English, and Dutch: from Middle High German and Middle English lappe, Middle Dutch lap ‘cloth, patch, rag’, a metonymic occupational name for a mender of clothes or shoes, or a nickname given to someone who wore ragged clothes or garments with loose folds, flaps, or hanging sleeves, or who had fleshy folds of skin on his face, neck, or body.

German: nickname for a simple-minded person, from Middle Low German lappe ‘fool’. This surname (in any of the two possible senses; see also 1 above) is also found in France (mainly Alsace, also Lorraine).
English: nickname from Anglo-Norman French lappe ‘burdock’, perhaps for someone with a prickly character.

nameMeanings.name: Tudor
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Welsh: from the personal name Tudur, a derivative of British Celtic Toutorīx, literally ‘people’ + ‘king’. It has sometimes been taken as a Welsh equivalent of Latin Theodorus, from Greek Theodōros ‘gift of God’ (see Theodore ), for which the usual Welsh form is Tewdwr.
Romanian and Croatian: from the personal name Tudor, a variant of Teodor (see Theodore ).

History: The Tudor royal family, English sovereigns from the accession of Henry VII in 1485 to the death of Elizabeth I in 1603, were of Welsh origin, being descended from Owain ap Maredudd ap Tudur, who held lands at Penmynydd in Anglesey.

variants.name: Tutor
meanings: Probably Welsh: variant of   Tudor  .

variants.name: Theodore
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French/Haitian Serge, Henri, Pierre, Alide, Andre, Antoine, Benoit, Berthony, Chantal, Daphnee, Edouard, Guilene.

French and Mauritian (Théodore); West Indian (in Haiti, Martinique, and Guadeloupe Théodore); English and Welsh: from a personal name derived from Greek Theodōros, a compound of theos ‘God’ + dōron ‘gift’, which was relatively popular in the Middle Ages because of its auspicious meaning. The name was borne by several early Christian saints. There was considerable confusion with the ancient Germanic name Theodoric (see Terry ). In Wales, the personal name Theodore was also adopted as an approximation to the Welsh name Tewdwr (see Tudor ). In North America, this surname has absorbed various other European cognates and their derivatives, e.g. Greek Theodorakis , Macedonian Todorovski, and Serbian Todorović (see Todorovic ).

variants.name: Tedder
meanings: English (Surrey): from the Middle English personal name Thedere, Teddere, which is either from the Old English personal name Thēodhere (from thēod ‘people’ + here ‘army’) or from the cognate ancient Germanic personal name Theudher, which seems to have been adopted by the Normans. Alternatively, perhaps it may be an occupational name from Middle English teddere ‘one who teds’, i.e. a person whose occupation was to spread mown grass for drying.
Welsh: variant of   Tudor  .

nameMeanings.name: Trevor
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Welsh and English: habitational name from any of numerous places in Wales, in particular the one near Denbigh, so named from Welsh tre(f) ‘homestead, settlement’ + a mutated form of mawr ‘large’.
Irish (Dublin and Down): variant of   Trower  .

variants.name: Trower
meanings: English (mainly Sussex and Surrey): variant of   Thrower  .
Irish (Leitrim): shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Treabhair ‘descendant of Treabhar’, a byname meaning ‘industrious, prudent’. The name has been Anglicized as Trevor .
Americanized form of German Trauer, a habitational name for someone from Trauen in Lower Saxony.

variants.name: Trover
meanings: Possibly Irish: from Trower, a shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Treabhair (see Trevor ).

variants.name: Traver
meanings: French: unexplained.
Catalan: habitational name from any of the minor places called Traver, mainly in La Garrotxa and L'Empordà districts.
English: variant of   Trevor  . This form of the surname is very rare in Britain and Ireland.

historicEvents.description: "Shropshire is first recorded in the Anglo-Saxon Chronicle annal for 1006.[25] The origin of the name is the Old English Scrobbesbyrigscīr, meaning ""Shrewsburyshire"", ""the shire of the fortified place in the scrublands"" (or ""shrubs"", the modern derivate). Salop is an old name for Shropshire, historically used as an abbreviated form for post or telegrams, it is thought to derive from the Anglo-French ""Salopesberia"". It is normally replaced by the more contemporary ""Shrops"" although Shropshire residents are still referred to as ""Salopians"".[26] Salop however, is also used as an alternative name for the county town, Shrewsbury,[26] which also shares the motto of Floreat Salopia. When a county council for the county was first established in 1889, it was called Salop County Council.[27] Following the Local Government Act 1972, Salop became the official name of the county. The name was not well-regarded locally however,[28] and a subsequent campaign led by a local councillor, John Kenyon, succeeded in having both t"

en.lifeSummary: When Lluddocca ap Tudor Trevor , Lord of Maelors and Oswestry was born about 0934, in Denbighshire, Wales, his father, King Tudor Trevor of Hereford, was 35 and his mother, Angharad ferch Hywel, was 35. He married Angharad ferch Iago in 0979, in Flint, Flintshire, Wales. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 1 daughter. He died in 1037, in Chirk, Denbighshire, Wales, United Kingdom, at the age of 104.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Lluddocca ap Tudor Trevor , Lord of Maelors and Oswestry est né environ 0934, à Denbighshire, Galles, son père, King Tudor Trevor of Hereford, avait 35 ans et sa mère, Angharad ferch Hywel, avait 35 ans. Il a épousé Angharad ferch Iago en 0979, à Flint, Flintshire, Galles. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé en 1037, à Chirk, Denbighshire, Pays de Galles, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 104 ans.

de.Llys Y, Flintshire, Wales: Llys Y Coed, Flintshire, Wales, United Kingdom
de.lifeSummary: Als Lluddocca ap Tudor Trevor , Lord of Maelors and Oswestry um 0934 in Denbighshire, Wales geboren wurde, war sein Vater King Tudor Trevor of Hereford 35 und seine Mutter Angharad ferch Hywel 35. Er hat 0979 in Flint, Flintshire, Wales Angharad ferch Iago geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er starb 1037 im Alter von 104 Jahren in Chirk, Denbighshire, Wales, United Kingdom.

ja.lifeSummary: Lluddocca ap Tudor Trevor , Lord of Maelors and Oswestryが約0934年にウェールズデンビグhシュアイアで生まれた時，父King Tudor Trevor of Herefordは35歳，母Angharad ferch Hywelは35歳であった。0979年にウェールズフリントシュアイアフリントでAngharad ferch Iagoと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は1037年にイギリスウェールズデンビグhシュアイアチルクで104歳で死去した。

ko.Llys Y, Flintshire, Wales: 영국 웨일스 플린츠히르 리스 와이 코드
ko.lifeSummary: Lluddocca ap Tudor Trevor , Lord of Maelors and Oswestry(이)가 대략 0934년 웨일스 덴비그h실르에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 King Tudor Trevor of Hereford(은)는 35세였고, 그의 어머니 Angharad ferch Hywel(은)는 35세였다. 그는 0979년 웨일스 플린츠히르 플린트에서 Angharad ferch Iago(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1037년 영국 웨일스 덴비그h실르 치르크에서 104세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Lluddocca ap Tudor Trevor , Lord of Maelors and Oswestry nasceu aproximadamente 0934, em Denbighshire, Gales, seu pai, King Tudor Trevor of Hereford, tinha 35 anos e sua mãe, Angharad ferch Hywel, tinha 35 anos. Ele casou-se com Angharad ferch Iago em 0979, em Flint, Flintshire, Gales. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 1 filha. Ele faleceu em 1037, em Chirk, Denbighshire, Gales, United Kingdom, com 104 anos.

ru.Llys Y, Flintshire, Wales: ллиз и коед, флинцхир, Уэльс, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Lluddocca ap Tudor Trevor , Lord of Maelors and Oswestry родился около 0934 г. в местности денбаишир, Уэльс, его отцу по имени King Tudor Trevor of Hereford было 35 лет, а матери, которую звали Angharad ferch Hywel, 35. Он женился на женщине по имени Angharad ferch Iago в 0979 г. в местности флинт, флинцхир, Уэльс. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 1 дочь. Он умер в 1037 г. в местности чирк, денбаишир, Уэльс, Великобритания в возрасте 104 лет.

Se casó con Angharad ferch Iago en 0979, en Flint, Flintshire, Gales. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 1 hija.
